analysis.overview: Mahila E-Haat is an online marketing platform launched by the Ministry of Women and Child Development to support women entrepreneurs, self-help groups, and NGOs in showcasing and selling their products and services directly to buyers. The initiative aims to strengthen the socio-economic empowerment of women by leveraging technology for market access, financial inclusion, and entrepreneurship development. It provides a digital marketplace where women can list their handmade, homemade, and homegrown products without any cost.
objectives: To provide an online marketing platform for women entrepreneurs, self-help groups, and NGOs
To enable direct interaction between women vendors and buyers
To promote financial inclusion and economic empowerment of women
To support the sale of handmade, homemade, and homegrown products through digital means
To strengthen women's participation in entrepreneurship and e-commerce
analysis.eligibility: The platform is open to Indian women entrepreneurs, self-help groups, and NGOs engaged in manufacturing, production, or sale of products and services. There is no registration fee or cost to list products on the portal.
analysis.benefits: Women entrepreneurs can list their products for free on the Mahila E-Haat portal, gain access to a national market, interact directly with buyers, receive online payments, and benefit from promotional support. The platform facilitates transactions without charging any commission or fee to the sellers.
analysis.financial_support: Mahila E-Haat does not provide direct financial support, grants, loans, or subsidies. It is a free online marketplace that enables women to sell their products and earn income through direct sales to buyers.
analysis.application_process: 1. Visit the Mahila E-Haat portal at https://www.mahilahaat.gov.in/ 2. Click on 'Register as a Seller' 3. Fill in the required details including name, contact information, address, and bank account details 4. Upload product images and descriptions 5. Submit the registration form for verification 6. Once approved, list products and start selling to buyers across India.
analysis.deadlines: Rolling basis — registration and product listing are open throughout the year.
analysis.implementing_agency: Ministry of Women and Child Development
caveats: The platform does not guarantee sales or fix prices for products
Sellers are responsible for product quality, delivery, and after-sales service
Mahila E-Haat facilitates only the connection between buyers and sellers; it does not involve itself in transactions
Only Indian women entrepreneurs, self-help groups, and NGOs are eligible to register
